frame_analyses.description: Title slide showing two foam limbs (Penrose drain models) on green surgical field with text 'LAPAROSCOPIC EXTRACORPOREAL KNOTS / TUMBLE SQUARE KNOT' and instructor attribution to 'MURAT AKBABA, MD'
Repeat title slide with slight variation in diagram overlay position
Hands visible holding two surgical instruments (needle drivers) manipulating dark suture material through white Penrose drain model on green field. Suture appears to be passing through drain marks.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hands-on demonstration of intracorporeal technique; bilateral grasper control visible; suture trajectory appears perpendicular to drain

frame_analyses.description: Continuation of suture driving through drain; right hand (dominant) appears to be leading needle passage through drain slit
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean needle trajectory; appropriate angle of entry

frame_analyses.description: Suture fully passed through both marks; both limbs of drain visible with black suture material crossing through slit
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture placement appears complete; no obvious deviation from marked entry/exit points

frame_analyses.description: Hands positioned to begin knot tying; suture ends being manipulated to form initial wrap for surgeon's knot (double throw). Right hand appears to be leading the wrap formation.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Setup for first throw; both hands visible managing suture ends

frame_analyses.description: First throw in progress; knot loop visible forming around suture base. Right hand leading, left hand supporting tension.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Double throw being formed; knot appears to be developing cleanly

frame_analyses.description: First knot throw appearing to tighten; visible loop constricting on suture material near drain slit
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot tension appears appropriate; clean formation without twisting

frame_analyses.description: Hands repositioning for second throw (single throw); left hand now appearing to lead the wrap, reversing from first throw hand dominance
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch evident; appropriate alternation between throws

frame_analyses.description: Second throw being tightened; suture limbs showing tension being applied to cinch knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean single throw; knot appears secure

frame_analyses.description: Right hand repositioning to lead third throw; suture limbs being manipulated for final single throw
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch back to right hand; appropriate sequence

frame_analyses.description: Third throw tightening; final knot cinching visible at drain slit; knot appearing secure and well-approximated
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean final throw; drain slit appears closed with no visible gap

frame_analyses.description: Final positioning showing tied knot at drain slit. Instructional text overlay states 'Both limbs should be tightened with graspers intracorporeally' highlighting proper technique
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Teaching point emphasized regarding bilateral grasper cinching

frame_analyses.description: Repeat of knot completion showing final knot in place at drain slit with proper approximation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot appears secure; drain slit shows good closure

frame_analyses.description: Closing credits slide showing instructor 'MURAT AKBABA, MD' with message 'Thank you for your interest'

task_specific_assessments.video_type_note: This is an instructional/expert demonstration video, NOT a trainee performance evaluation. It is created by Dr. Murat Akbaba for educational purposes.

knot_assessments.notes: Clean double throw; appropriate tension; knot formation smooth
Proper hand switch; clean single throw

suture_placement.notes: Demonstration shows clean needle passage; no observable deviation from marked entry/exit points

drain_assessment.notes: Final knot shows complete approximation of drain slit with no visible gap

score_components.reason: This is an expert instructional/demonstration video, not a trainee performance. Per rubric classification, instructional videos with narration, overlays, and teaching content are NOT scored for performance metrics. Score calculation is not applicable.

confidence_rationale: Video classification is definitively instructional/expert demo. Title cards, instructor attribution, teaching overlay text ('Both limbs should be tightened with graspers intracorporeally'), and closing credits clearly establish this as educational content rather than a scored performance. Knot techniques and drain handling are clearly visible and executed expertly, but no timing or penalty assessment is appropriate for instructional material.
cannot_determine: Exact completion time (video is segmented and edited for instruction, not continuous task performance)
Suture cut phase (not shown in video)
Whether this represents a single continuous task or multiple demonstration angles

demonstrated_techniques: Proper intracorporeal needle passage through marked entry and exit points on Penrose drain
Surgeon's knot (double throw) formation with right hand leading
Hand alternation between successive throws (right → left → right)
Bilateral grasper management for knot cinching
Tension application for secure knot approximation
verbal_cues: Title identifies technique as 'Laparoscopic Extracorporeal Knots / Tumble Square Knot'
Teaching overlay: 'Both limbs should be tightened with graspers intracorporeally' — emphasizing bilateral control during knot cinching
common_errors_addressed: Unilateral grasper use (emphasized need for bilateral intracorporeal tightening)
Improper hand switching (demonstrates correct alternation pattern)
Loose or gaping knots (shows complete drain slit approximation as target)
teaching_content.instructor_credentials: Dr. Murat Akbaba, MD (indicated in video credits)

video_summary: This is an expert-led educational demonstration of intracorporeal suturing and knot tying on a Penrose drain model. Dr. Murat Akbaba demonstrates the proper sequence of a surgeon's knot followed by two alternating single throws, with emphasis on bilateral grasper control and complete drain slit approximation. The video uses title cards, instructional overlays, and closing credits to establish its educational intent. Performance-based FLS scoring is not applicable; the value of this video is in technique instruction and skill modeling.
improvement_suggestions: Not applicable — this is an expert demonstration, not a trainee evaluation
